name: Jay Inslee
subject: Does he think this is a joke?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
McConnell thinks he's being funny -- but climate change isn't something to joke about.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a111/2f5ea88d/78f2d0bd/2cbde322/496051363/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/climate-change-is-not-a-game?utm_source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&utm_campaign=green-new-deal-vote&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Our work to defeat climate change is taking hold. The Green New Deal has so much momentum that now the Senate Majority Leader is trying to take a swipe at it by bringing the resolution to a Senate vote. Friend, Senator McConnell thinks he's playing a clever game. He thinks it is bad politics to support bold, audacious proposals to defeat climate change. But look:**Climate change is not a game.** So my response to Senator McConnell is simple: We're ready for real climate action now. I'm thrilled that Senator Markey and Representative Ocasio-Cortez are putting the existential threat of our lifetime on the agenda in D.C. It's about time we get down to work on combating climate change -- and Senator McConnell can't distract us from our main goal by treating it like a joke. [**Join me in telling Senator McConnell right now: Climate change isn't a game -- it's time to take this seriously.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a111/2f5ea88d/78f2d0bd/2cbde322/496051363/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/climate-change-is-not-a-game?utm_source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&utm_campaign=green-new-deal-vote&utm_medium=ema") Taking action to address climate change can't stop with the Green New Deal. We need real, solid climate policy that makes a difference. That's why I have no problem allowing senators to vote on the Green New Deal. What Senator McConnell thinks is a game of chicken is actually a powerful tool to put climate front and center on the national stage. [**Tell Senator McConnell now: No climate games, real action on climate change now!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a111/2f5ea88d/78f2d0bd/2cbde322/496051363/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/climate-change-is-not-a-game?utm_source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&utm_campaign=green-new-deal-vote&utm_medium=ema") It's time to give our future the focus it needs. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a111/2f5ea88d/78f2d0bd/2cbde322/496051363/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/climate-change-is-not-a-game?utm_source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&source=ema_2019-02-14_green-new-deal-vote&utm_campaign=green-new-deal-vote&utm_medium=ema")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump on 'Solid Legal Ground' to End the Crisis on our Southern Border
body:
"President Trump will have the law on his side when he uses the power of his office to secure the U.S. border with Mexico." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1dec2a776b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump on 'Solid Legal Ground' to End the Crisis on our Southern Border**

“President Trump will have the law on his side when he uses the power of his office to secure the U.S. border with Mexico,” writes former U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Joseph DiGenova in the _New York Daily News_.

“The current political reality in Washington ensures that the crisis on our southern border will continue to go unresolved unless the executive branch steps in. Fortunately, both the Constitution and the Congress have placed the authority to do so firmly with the President of the United States.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c481850445&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=43d46e62c5&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Paul Bedard reports that “Congress approved historic funding levels for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and border patrol, added miles to the border wall, and increased the cap on criminal illegal immigrants that can be jailed. Insiders who worked on the deal said that compared to going along with a spending continuing resolution, the GOP scored several wins.”
---
|
---
| Yesterday, First Lady Melania Trump “brought Valentine’s Day cards to young patients being treated at the Children’s Inn at the National Institutes of Health” and spent the afternoon making arts and crafts with about a dozen children and their siblings, Caitlin Yilek reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ce35902d61&e=3ee4985eea)_. The First Lady “was gifted a silver necklace inscribed with ‘faith’ and ‘hope’ from Amani, a 13-year-old from Kenya who has sickle cell disease and is preparing for a bone marrow transplant. Amani has a bracelet to match the necklace he gave the first lady.”
---
|
---
| The _[Bloomberg](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a943c5ee31&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes that President Trump is launching a smart artificial intelligence plan “to ensure the U.S. keeps up in an increasingly crucial industry.” The President’s plan, “dubbed the ‘American AI Initiative,’ is a measured response [that] directs federal agencies to invest more in artificial-intelligence R&D, share data and computer models with outside researchers, establish clear technical standards, boost workforce development, and otherwise prioritize the technology.”
---
|
---
| In _[USA Today](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a8540dbd88&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Daniel Di Martino, a Venezuelan expatriate, writes that America must resist the socialist policies that destroyed his home country. “In his recent State of the Union address, President Donald Trump said: ‘The United States will never be a socialist country.’ I sincerely hope the president is right, and that every American can resist the lure of false promises.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e47394ab06&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=75183abbf4&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9d62450ebb)

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: A real national emergency
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
Sign the petition and help us fight.
|
---

| | Peter -- In just the last year, 1,200 kids have been shot -- too often in communities of color that get ignored. Gun violence is a real national emergency. President Trump's own tax law encourages companies like GM to close factories, eliminate jobs, and ship work overseas, while thousands of Americans pay the price. That's a real national emergency. The opioid epidemic has claimed thousands of lives in Ohio, killing 11 people every day. President Trump called that a national emergency, but has done very little about it. Trump says he doesn't believe climate change is real, even though it is one of the greatest moral threats we face. Climate change is a real national emergency. Bridges across this country are structurally deficient. Roads are in need of repair. Communities are being poisoned by their own water. That's a real emergency. More than a million hardworking truck drivers, miners, and others are at risk of losing the retirement they earned because of Wall Street greed. That's an emergency. As a country, these are real emergencies we face every day. Trump does nothing about them. But now, like a child who again has failed to get his way, he is trying to ignore the Constitution to build his unnecessary wall. **Donald Trump is the real national emergency.** [**If you agree, make your voice heard now.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc16/4462dc26/8ef83655/201ed656/400598422/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190215") With gratitude, Sherrod

#
| | Yesterday, Trump announced plans to declare a national emergency to build his border wall. Kamala sent an urgent email asking you to sign her petition against the wall -- **will you join the tens of thousands of people who made their voices heard and add your name, too?** [**Add your name to Kamala's petition to oppose the President's decision to declare a national emergency to build his border wall.**](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/no-border-wall?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190215-lift-full&source=ema190215-lift-full&t=1&akid=3094%2E5755789%2E3IlBt9) | | [Sign the petition](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/no-border-wall?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190215-lift-full&source=ema190215-lift-full&t=2&akid=3094%2E5755789%2E3IlBt9)
---
* * *
**_Original message from Kamala Harris, February 14, 2019_**
Just a few moments ago, Mitch McConnell announced on the Senate floor that President Trump will sign an emergency declaration in order to secure funding for his useless and costly border wall.
This comes after both Congress and the American people have repeatedly made clear that they do not support building this wall.
**I want to be clear with you -- this is ridiculous and I am going to do everything in my power to stop Trump from building this wall. It’s a waste of your money and it won’t make our country safer.**
But I cannot do it alone. I need you with me in this fight:
**[If you’re with me, add your name to my urgent petition opposing the President’s decision to declare a national emergency in a blatantly political attempt to build his vanity project.](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/no-border-wall?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190215-lift-full&source=ema190215-lift-full&t=3&akid=3094%2E5755789%2E3IlBt9)**

---
We should be doing something about the actual emergencies plaguing our nation, like the gun violence epidemic or health care access, not playing politics in order to build a ridiculously wasteful border wall.
I am ready to fight with everything I have. I hope you’re with me.
— Kamala

subject: Indiana -> DC
body:
We’ve been saying it for years. Things like, “If only they did it like this in Washington,” or “let’s take the Indiana way to DC.” And we're...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/indiana-dc?e=1acd9bb3d6)
http://indiana.gop/2019springdinner?mc_cid=a836a7c581&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Indiana -> DC

We’ve been saying it for years. Things like, “If only they did it like this in Washington,” or “let’s take the Indiana way to DC.”

And we’re making real progress. You probably won’t hear it on the evening news, but under the leadership of President Donald Trump, Vice President Mike Pence and the battalion of Hoosiers we’ve sent to Washington, since President Trump’s election we’ve seen:
* 5.3 million new American jobs
* 600,000 new manufacturing jobs
* Wages rising at the fastest pace in decades
* More Americans working than ever before!

And this week, big news broke that our very own Governor Eric Holcomb has been picked as one of just two governors in the entire country to serve on a board – co-chaired by Ivanka Trump – that will advise the Trump Administration on ways to make sure our American workforce is skilled-up to the 21st century jobs of today and tomorrow. He’ll be taking the Hoosier solutions we’re building in workforce development to the national stage (more on this below)!

Democrats are trying to obstruct this progress at every turn. Just look at how extreme liberals like Nancy Pelosi and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have been acting lately. But when it comes to results, there’s no doubt that the Indiana way is delivering results in Washington.

-Kyle Hupfer
GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS
https://holcombforindiana.com/policy-advisory-board/?mc_cid=a836a7c581&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Governor Holcomb Picked for Trump Workforce Board

This week, Governor Eric Holcomb was appointed to serve on President Donald Trump’s new American Workforce Policy Advisory Board.

The board, which will be co-chaired by Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump and Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross, will play an important role in helping make sure all Americans can participate in today’s booming economy. Governor Holcomb and the board will provide advice and recommendations to top leaders in the Trump Administration on strategies to combat the nation’s skills crisis and better prepare the American workforce for 21st century jobs -- the ones available now, and the ones still in the works.

Click here to learn more (https://holcombforindiana.com/policy-advisory-board/?mc_cid=a836a7c581&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) about how Indiana is laser-focused on preparing the Hoosier workforce for today’s high-wage, in-demand jobs, and how Governor Holcomb will be serving on this board alongside the CEOs of major U.S. companies like Apple, Walmart, Lockheed Martin, Home Depot and Siemens!
